report_name: Hacking Group 'FlamingChina' Claims 10 Petabyte Military Data Heist from Chinese Supercomputer

threat_actors.name: FlamingChina
threat_actors.description: A previously unknown hacking entity that claimed responsibility for a colossal data breach targeting a Chinese supercomputer, allegedly stealing 10 petabytes of sensitive military data and offering it for sale.
threat_actors.goals: Sale of allegedly stolen sensitive military data, including weapon schematics and simulations, for financial profit.

identities.name: Aviation Industry Corporation of China
identities.description: A Chinese state-run defense and technology institution allegedly targeted by the FlamingChina group, from which sensitive military data was reportedly stolen.

identities.name: National University of Defense Technology
identities.description: A Chinese state-run technology institution allegedly targeted by the FlamingChina group, from which sensitive military data was reportedly stolen.

indicators.name: Data Archiving and Encryption via Command Line
indicators.pattern:
title: Data Archiving and Encryption via Command Line
id: 2b1f8c7a-9d0e-4b3c-8a1f-5d6e7f8a9b0c
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of 'tar' to archive files piped directly to 'openssl' for encryption. This pattern is often used for staging and obfuscating data prior to exfiltration.
references:
  - https://www.scmagazine.com/brief/report-us-accounts-for-most-plcs-subjected-to-iranian-targeting
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-11
tags:
  - attack.collection
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    CommandLine|re: 'tar .*\|.*openssl enc'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects a command-line pattern where 'tar' is used to archive data and pipe it directly to 'openssl' for encryption, a technique for staging data for exfiltration.

relationships.source_name: Data Archiving and Encryption via Command Line
